channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: New Jersey Democrat proposes bill to create travel advisories to inform pregnant women of state abortion laws
item.description: A New Jersey Democrat proposed a bill to create travel advisories to notify women of restrictive abortion laws in other states they may be visiting.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: From Walz’s hometown to an American Legion, Minnesotans react to governor in VP debate
item.description: Supporters of the Republican and Democratic presidential tickets gathered Tuesday evening for debate watch parties around Minnesota for the likely only debate between Sen. J.D. Vance of Ohio and Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik and Matt Sepic, MPR News

item.title: In VP debate, Walz acknowledges he wasn’t in China during Tiananmen uprising
item.description: APM Reports, the investigations and documentaries unit of MPR News, first reported that Walz’s claim during a 2014 congressional hearing on the 25th anniversary of the massacre didn’t square up with media accounts in 1989.
item.dc:creator: Tom Scheck, Curtis Gilbert, Caspar von Au, and Forest Hunt, MPR News

item.title: Walz and Vance go in depth on policy while attacking each other’s running mates in VP debate
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — In a debate that evoked a calmer era in American politics, Tim Walz and JD Vance on Tuesday went after each other's running mates and sought to shore up their campaigns' vulnerabilities at a time of renewed fears of a regional war in the Middle East and sadness over devastation from
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, CHRIS MEGERIAN, WILL WEISSSERT and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fact-check fight: CBS News cuts candidates' mics after JD Vance objects to a moderator's correction
item.description: CBS News briefly muted the debate microphones of the two vice presidential candidates after Republican JD Vance objected to a fact-check made by Margaret Brennan, one of the network's moderators.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After pressing an Israel-Hezbollah cease-fire, the Biden administration shifts its message
item.description: The Biden administration says there's a significant difference between Israeli actions that have expanded its war against the Iranian-backed militant groups Hamas and Hezbollah and Iran’s retaliatory missile attack against Israel.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Lee And Lolita C. Baldor, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pennsylvania town grapples with Trump assassination attempt ahead of his return
item.description: BUTLER, Pa. (AP) — Donald Trump is going back to Butler, where the world last saw him pump his fist and beseech followers to “fight,” even as blood streaked his face from a would-be assassin's bullet.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans are more likely than Democrats to see Israel as a US ally: AP-NORC poll
item.description: A new Pearson Institute/AP-NORC survey finds Republicans are more likely to see Israel as an ally that shares the U.S.'s interests and values, while Democrats are more likely to see Israel as a partner that doesn’t share U.S. interests and values.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Some of the most notable quotes from the JD Vance-Tim Walz vice presidential debate
item.description: The debate between Ohio Senator JD Vance and Minnesota Governor Tim Walz featured an often orderly, policy-focused and even civil dialogue between the two men seeking to serve as the next president’s second in command.
item.dc:creator: Matt Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance called Harris a 'border czar' during the VP debate. What does that mean?
item.description: Republicans, including former President Donald Trump and his 2024 running mate, Senator J.D. Vance of Ohio, have increasingly referred to Vice President Kamala Harris as a failed "border czar.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz misleadingly claims to have been in Hong Kong during period tied to Tiananmen Square massacre
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Multiple news reports indicate that Democratic vice presidential nominee Minnesota Gov.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Walz on Tiananmen Square screw-up: 'I'm a knucklehead at times'
item.description: Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz (D), pressed at the vice presidential debate over false statements he made in which he said he was present during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989, said: “I’m a knucklehead at times.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance and Walz keep it civil in a policy-heavy discussion: VP debate takeaways
item.description: Vice presidential nominees JD Vance and Tim Walz have used their time on the debate stage to focus the bulk of their attacks not on one another, but on those at the top of their rival tickets.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Zeke Miller And Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump rips Walz, CBS moderators in debate's opening 15 minutes
item.description: “Walz is taking so many notes - Never seen a Candidate take more! He needs the notes to keep his brain intact,” Trump wrote.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump downplays troop injuries in 2020 missile strike: 'You mean because they had a headache?'
item.description: "So first of all, injured. What does injured mean? Injured means — you mean because they had a headache? Because the bombs never hit the fort," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: School board to pay $575K to teacher fired for not using transgender student's pronouns
item.description: A Virginia school board has agreed to pay a former high school teacher more than $500,000 in damages and attorneys' fees as part of a legal settlement after the instructor's dismissal in 2018 for refusing to use a transgender student's pronouns.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump won't participate in interview for '60 Minutes' election special
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — CBS News said Tuesday that Republican presidential nominee Donald Trump has declined to participate in an interview with “60 Minutes” for its election special, which will go forward next Monday with Democratic opponent Kamala Harris alone.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP leaders press Biden administration to get tougher on Iran after missile attack
item.description: Republican leaders on Capitol Hill are calling on President Biden to get tougher on Iran in the wake of Tuesday’s missile attack on Israel, urging the administration to adopt a series of specific measures designed to cripple the military powers of Tehran and its proxies.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson says Congress has provided funds for Helene response
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Tuesday said Congress has already authorized the disaster relief funds needed for the immediate response to Helene, the storm that has ravaged multiple states from Florida up through western North Carolina and Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump blames Biden, Harris for Iran's attack on Israel: 'Very close to global catastrophe'
item.description: Former President Donald Trump slammed President Biden and Vice President Kamala Harris after Iran launched an unprecedented ballistic missiles attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: U.S. Fish and Wildlife signs Tamarac co-stewardship agreement with White Earth
item.description: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service and the White Earth Nation have signed a co-stewardship agreement for the Tamarac National Wildlife Refuge in northwestern Minnesota. The agreement will not change public access to the refuge, but will give tribal resource managers more input in refuge management.
item.dc:creator: Dan Gunderson, MPR News

item.title: Cook Political Report shifts Texas Senate race toward Democrats
item.description: The Cook Political Report on Tuesday shifted Texas’s Senate race from "likely Republican" to "lean Republican," signaling momentum for Rep. Colin Allred's (D) challenge against incumbent Sen. Ted Cruz (R) in the red state.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Why Tuesday's vice presidential debate could matter more than history suggests
item.description: Tim Walz and JD Vance might have the chance to reshape the political landscape Tuesday in their first and only debate.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans eager to keep House control are open to Trump’s SALT reversal
item.description: Republicans are open to raising the state and local tax (SALT) deduction cap that the party championed and put in place under former President Trump seven years ago as they aim to win total control of government in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrat calls for Congress to return to pass disaster aid after Hurricane Helene
item.description: Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D-Md.) on Tuesday called for the Senate to return from its lengthy preelection recess to pass a disaster aid package following the damage left in multiple states by Hurricane Helene. “I think we should pass it yesterday.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Get to know the candidates: We sent participants in the 22nd Congressional District Debate a questionnaire. Here’s how they responded
item.description: Each candidate participating in Wednesday's 22nd Congressional Debate responded to our questionnaire.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Gutierrez, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris will tour Helene devastation in Georgia, North Carolina as storm scrambles campaign schedule
item.description: The Democratic nominee plans to visit Georgia on Wednesday and survey the devastation in North Carolina in the coming days, according to aides.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance, Tim Walz face off in 2024 VP debate: Key takeaways
item.description: Republican J.D. Vance and Democrat Tim Walz met Tuesday in the lone vice presidential debate of the 2024 election, bringing together undercards who have spent two months going after each other and the opposing nominees who top the major-party tickets.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump edges Harris in North Carolina poll, state that hasn't voted Democratic since 2008
item.description: It's not clear how the devastation of Hurricane Helene will affect the presidential race in North Carolina, where former President Trump maintains a razor-thin lead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP leader says Vance 'absolutely prepared' for VP debate showdown with Walz
item.description: Republican National Committee chair Michael Whatley is confident that Sen. JD Vance of Ohio is "absolutely prepared" for Tuesday night's vice presidential debate showdown.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump speaks in Wisconsin ahead of VP debate
item.description: The former president criticized Kamala Harris and Joe Biden for their handling of the conflict in the Middle East and the border.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Unearthed photo shows Tim Walz appointee decorated house with posters of murderous communist dictators
item.description: Ida Rukavina, appointed by Minnesota Gov. Tim Walz to lead the state's Department of Iron Range Resources and Rehabilitation, posted a photo on social media in 2021 showing her walls decorated with communist imagery.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gillibrand vs. Sapraicone: The Race for US Senate forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand and Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone faced off in a Senate forum that aired on PIX11 News Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: Here are the rules for the Vance-Walz debate
item.description: The debate, which will kick off at 9 p.m. EDT on Tuesday from the network’s New York office, will be moderated by CBS anchor Norah O’Donnell and "Face the Nation" host Margaret Brennan.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Senate candidates Gillibrand, Sapraicone to face off in PIX11 forum
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Election Day is just a few weeks away. Do you know who you’re voting for in the New York race for U.S. Senate? Republican candidate Mike Sapraicone is battling to unseat Democratic Sen. Kirsten Gillibrand.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Cook, PIX 11

item.title: Eric Adams pleads not guilty in federal bribery case
item.description: LOWER MANHATTAN, Manhattan (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams pleaded not guilty to federal charges Friday afternoon in a Lower Manhattan courthouse. Adams, who was not handcuffed and remained seated for the proceedings, told the judge he read the entire indictment. “I’m not guilty, your honor,” he said.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, PIX 11

item.title: Secret Service agent placed on leave after 'misconduct allegation' with Harris staffer
item.description: The U.S. Secret Service recently notified Vice President Kamala Harris’ office about an incident involving one of its agents and a “misconduct allegation,” according to spokespeople for both the USSS and the vice president's office.
item.dc:creator: Monica Alba and Kelly O'Donnell, WFLA, NBC
